summary: Matched the Togepi Pokémon item source by replacing asm/melee/it/items/ittogepy.s with a C implementation in src/melee/it/items/ittogepy.c, updating the build to compile the C object, expanding the local item header declarations, and adding the missing x18 field to itPokemonAttributes needed by the random state-selection logic. The PR had no body or review comments, so intent is inferred from the title and diff.
what_changed: Deleted the 385-line hand-written assembly file asm/melee/it/items/ittogepy.s.
Added a 181-line C implementation for ittogepy, including the ItemStateTable it_803F8058 and functions it_802D36B0 through it_802D3AF0.
Changed configure.py entry for melee/it/items/ittogepy.c from NonMatching to Matching.
Changed obj_files.mk to build src/melee/it/items/ittogepy.c.o instead of asm/melee/it/items/ittogepy.s.o.
Expanded src/melee/it/items/ittogepy.h with platform/item includes and prototypes for all newly implemented local functions.
Added s32 x18 to itPokemonAttributes in src/melee/it/itCommonItems.h, matching the extra probability/threshold field used by Togepi's random state selection.
Adjusted config/GALE01/symbols.txt for the rodata symbol at 0x803B86F8 from global it_803B86F8 size 0x10 to local @183 size 0xC.
Cleaned up src/melee/it/items/itpippi.c includes by removing unused ef headers and adding <baselib/gobj.h>.
smart_moves: Kept address-based symbol names such as it_802D36B0 and it_803F8058, minimizing naming risk while the surrounding item code remains largely address-oriented.
Modeled the assembly state table directly as an ItemStateTable array, preserving state IDs 0 through 6 and their callback layout.
Used the shared itPokemonAttributes struct instead of introducing a Togepi-only attribute struct, but extended it with x18 when the assembly showed an additional 0x18 field read.
Preserved item transition behavior with existing helpers such as Item_80268E5C, Item_80268D34, it_8026E15C, it_8026D62C, it_80272860, and it_8027A118.
Included PAD_STACK(8) in it_802D3848, indicating an explicit stack-shape tactic was needed for matching.
Updated all required integration points together: source file, object list, matching status, header prototypes, shared struct, and symbol map.
decomp_lessons: For Pokémon item matches, verify special attribute struct width against every load in the assembly; here x18 was required for the final random threshold bucket.
When replacing an asm item with C, update both configure.py matching status and obj_files.mk object selection in the same PR.
State-table conversion is often a reliable anchor: translate the .data callback table first, then implement functions to satisfy the table signatures.
Hitlag callbacks may need to be explicitly nulled after state changes; this implementation repeatedly sets entered_hitlag and exited_hitlag to NULL after Item_80268E5C transitions.
Small rodata symbol corrections may be part of matching a file; this PR converted it_803B86F8 into local @183 and corrected its size to 0xC.
Include hygiene matters for matching and type correctness: C item files that use Item_GObj/HSD_GObj may need <baselib/gobj.h> instead of unrelated effect headers.
naming_conventions: Unidentified functions retained address-style names: it_802D36B0, it_802D3728, it_802D372C, it_802D374C, it_802D37C0, it_802D37FC, it_802D3848, it_802D390C, it_802D3970, it_802D39AC, it_802D39F8, it_802D3A68, it_802D3A8C, it_802D3AF0.
Unidentified global state table retained address-style name it_803F8058.

Struct fields follow existing offset-style placeholders such as xC4_article_data, x4_specialAttributes, xDD4_itemVar.pokemon.timer, xDAC_itcmd_var0, xDB0_itcmd_var1, xDB4_itcmd_var2, xD0_itemStateDesc, and xCC_item_attr.
The newly added itPokemonAttributes field uses placeholder offset naming x18, consistent with nearby xC, x10, and x14 fields.
Local rodata in symbols.txt can use generated local labels like @183 when a formerly named global is not truly global.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Recreated the assembly's random branch ladder in it_802D3848 using HSD_Randi(sa->max), cumulative thresholds sa->xC, sa->x10, sa->x14, and sa->x18, and selected states 2 through 6.
Used PAD_STACK(8) in it_802D3848 to reproduce stack layout.
Matched void no-op callback it_802D3728 as an explicit return-only function.
Matched airborne/grounded callback choice by branching on ground_or_air == GA_Air and calling it_8026E15C for air or it_8026D62C for ground with it_802D3728 as callback.
Matched physics updates by calling it_80272860 with x10_fall_speed and x14_fall_speed_max only when the item is airborne.
Preserved state animation updates with Item_80268E5C(gobj, state, ITEM_ANIM_UPDATE) rather than open-coded constants except where the original state IDs are still visible.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
follow_up_queries: Find other Pokémon item PRs that extend itPokemonAttributes or use x18 probability thresholds.
Search for PRs matching itmew, ithitodeman, or ithouou to compare Togepi state-table patterns.
Look for uses of PAD_STACK in item decomp matches to identify recurring stack-shape fixes.
Audit nearby rodata symbols it_803B86BC through it_803B8724 for local/global size corrections.
